source_tasks.title: Official and FIA Context
query_templates: British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 Formula 1 official preview classification
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 FIA documents race director notes
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 penalties grid changes FIA
acceptance_checks: Source is Formula1.com, FIA.com, or a linked official document.
Publication and observation timestamps are at or before the cutoff.
Classification, penalties, or rules claims include the governing document or official page.
rejection_rules: Reject fan summaries when the official document is available.
Reject any post-cutoff classification or penalty update for replay cutoffs.

source_tasks.title: Team Upgrades and Track Fit
query_templates: British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 team upgrades Mercedes Ferrari McLaren Red Bull Racing
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 track characteristics high_speed F1
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 Mercedes Ferrari McLaren Red Bull preview
acceptance_checks: Prefer team releases, named team principal quotes, or established outlets quoting named staff.

Tie every claimed effect to a metric such as race_pace, power_unit, energy_recovery, straight_line_speed, drag_efficiency, low_speed_traction, weight, upgrade_effect, tyre_deg, strategy, or reliability.
Use small magnitude unless the source says the part or issue is event-specific and already run-tested.
rejection_rules: Reject unsourced upgrade rumors as model inputs; store only as review_required rumor claims if needed.
Reject generic optimism unless it maps to a specific car, circuit, weather, or reliability mechanism.

source_tasks.title: Weather and Track Conditions
query_templates: British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 weather forecast race rain wind track temperature
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 circuit weather radar F1
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 qualifying race forecast
acceptance_checks: Weather claim states forecast window, race session timing, rain probability, and wind or temperature when available.
Forecast source is captured at or before the knowledge cutoff.
Wet or wind claims are translated into wet_skill, strategy, reliability, or qualifying_pace effects.
rejection_rules: Reject vague 'rain possible' claims without timing or probability.
Reject forecasts updated after the cutoff unless historical archive proof is attached.

source_tasks.title: Structured Session and Form Data
query_templates: OpenF1 British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 laps stints weather race control
FastF1 British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 session results lap times
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 long run pace tyre degradation F1
acceptance_checks: Structured data claims include session, metric, and cutoff availability.
Practice claims distinguish low-fuel headline pace from long-run race pace.
Race result claims are only used when the prediction cutoff is after the race.
rejection_rules: Reject practice fastest-lap headlines as race pace unless stint context is available.
Reject same-event race result data for pre-race prediction cutoffs.

source_tasks.title: Market Rules and Snapshot Eligibility
query_templates: British Grand Prix Polymarket winner market rules 2026-07-05
British Grand Prix Polymarket F1 podium pole fastest lap market
British Grand Prix prediction market final classification rules
acceptance_checks: Market rule claims identify resolution source, cancellation handling, and post-race change handling.
Price data is not embedded as evidence; it must enter through MarketSnapshot ingestion.
Candidate market season, event, and outcome mapping are unambiguous.
rejection_rules: Reject mismatched season markets even if the race name matches.
Reject price screenshots or prose odds as formal market snapshots.

source_tasks.title: Independent Corroboration
query_templates: British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 F1 paddock notes named reporting
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 race preview upgrades reliability named sources
British Grand Prix 2026-07-05 driver interviews team quotes
acceptance_checks: Media claim has named outlet, author or agency, and a publication timestamp.
High-impact claims are corroborated by official, team, structured-data, or second independent media source.
Conflicting sources are captured as separate claims with review_required=true.
rejection_rules: Reject social reposts, anonymous rumors, and aggregation pages as standalone high-confidence evidence.
Reject claims where the original source cannot be identified.

impact_bands.use_when: Context is directionally relevant but unlikely to change simulation ordering.
impact_bands.review_rule: May be archived without review only when source reliability is at least 0.75.

impact_bands.use_when: Single-source setup, weather, or form signal with plausible but limited race effect.
impact_bands.review_rule: Review required when source reliability is below 0.70 or timestamps are uncertain.

impact_bands.use_when: Source-backed event-specific issue, upgrade, penalty, or weather signal likely to move a target group.
impact_bands.review_rule: Needs source linkage plus either official/team/structured source or independent corroboration.

impact_bands.use_when: Confirmed grid penalty, major reliability issue, substantial rain change, or run-tested upgrade effect.
impact_bands.review_rule: Always review; require at least one high-reliability source and explicit reasoning.

race_pace.notes: Use for clean-air or long-run speed. Do not use headline practice fastest laps alone.

qualifying_pace.notes: Use for low-fuel or grid-position signals; keep separate from race pace.

tyre_deg.notes: Positive direction means better tyre management or lower degradation.

reliability.notes: Positive direction means lower DNF or penalty risk.

wet_skill.notes: Event-level weather changes wet probability; driver-level claims affect relative wet performance.

strategy.notes: Use for pit-loss, safety-car optionality, compound constraints, or settlement interpretation.

power_unit.notes: Use for combustion/turbo/power-unit strength. The simulator weights this more on power and high-speed tracks.

energy_recovery.notes: Use for battery, harvesting, and clipping claims. Positive means stronger deployment or less clipping.

straight_line_speed.notes: Use for straight-line pace that is not purely power-unit reliability. Weighted higher on power/high-speed circuits.

drag_efficiency.notes: Positive means better speed for a given downforce; negative captures draggy packages or clipping-sensitive setups.

low_speed_traction.notes: Use for starts, traction zones, and slow-corner exits. Weighted higher on street and low-speed tracks.

weight.notes: Positive means lighter or closer to minimum weight; negative means overweight or ballast-related loss.

upgrade_effect.notes: Use only when a specific upgrade is linked to observed or stated performance. Keep review_required for rumors.

quality_gates: Codex must not emit final probabilities or direct trading recommendations.
Every claim must link to a snapshotted source URL and claim id in source_log.json.
published_at and observed_at must be at or before knowledge_cutoff unless the claim is rejected.
A late local snapshot needs cutoff-valid historical_archive proof before it can support formal replay.
Claims with source reliability below 0.70, unknown publication time, source conflict, or material impact must set review_required=true.
Technical claims must state the mechanism and circuit context before using power_unit, energy_recovery, drag_efficiency, low_speed_traction, weight, or upgrade_effect.
Magnitude must stay within the impact band justified by source quality and corroboration.
Market prices must enter through MarketSnapshot ingestion, not through Codex evidence claims.
tool_workflow: Run `python -m f1predict.cli prepare-research --event british_gp --knowledge-cutoff 2026-06-30T12:00:00+00:00` if workspace files are missing.
Use the source tasks in this plan to search web/FIA/team/weather/market sources.
Fill data/research/<event_id>/research_packet_template.json with inspected sources and normalized claims.
Attach historical_archive proof for any source inspected after the replay cutoff.
Run `python -m f1predict.cli prediction-packet --event british_gp --knowledge-cutoff 2026-06-30T12:00:00+00:00 --iterations 1200 --write` before discussing edge quality.
